data.title: Triumph Financial 10-Q: $102.8M Interest Income, $0.23 Diluted EPS
data.description:
Triumph Financial reported stable total interest income of $102.8 million for the quarter and returned to profitability with diluted earnings per share of $0.23, driven by higher fee income and improved operating results. Net income was $6.4 million (or $5.6 million available to common stockholders after $0.8 million of preferred dividends). The quarter also saw growth in noninterest income and margin improvements across the factoring business as the company shifts further toward a payments and fee-based model.

Financial Highlights

  • Total interest income: $102.8 million, largely stable year-over-year (up $0.5 million).
  • Total noninterest income: $19.7 million, up $2.5 million (14.6%) driven by higher fee income.
  • Net income: $6.4 million, an increase of $6.3 million year-over-year (from $0.02 million).
  • Net income available to common stockholders: $5.6 million (after $0.8 million in preferred dividends).
  • Diluted earnings per share: $0.23, compared with $(0.03) in the prior year period.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix shift: Continued strategic shift from balance-sheet lending to a payments network model following the HubTran acquisition, increasing fee-based revenue and processed invoice volumes.
  • Transportation segment growth: Transportation revenue rose 23.5% year-over-year, supported by Factoring and Payments businesses.
  • Factoring momentum: Factored receivables grew 17.4% year-over-year with higher invoice volume and average invoice size, contributing to a materially improved Factoring operating margin.
  • Payments performance: Payments revenue grew 27% year-over-year on an ex-intersegment basis, and processed invoices increased by 14.5%.
  • New offerings and M&A: Launched an Intelligence business and completed the acquisition of Greenscreens, adding subscription and pricing data revenue streams and cross-sell opportunities.
  • Cost and efficiency actions: Executed approximately a 5% workforce reduction and other cost initiatives, realizing technology-driven efficiencies and rebalancing the cost structure.

Original SEC Filing: Triumph Financial, Inc. [ TFIN ] - 10-Q - Apr. 21, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: PREAX 10-Q: $0 Revenue, EPS $(0.02) for Six Months
data.description:
PREAX reported no revenue for the three and six months ended November 30, 2025, and posted a net loss of $(0.112) million for the quarter and $(0.857) million for the six-month period, resulting in basic and diluted loss per share of $(0.00) for the quarter and $(0.02) for six months. The company’s results reflect increased general and administrative and research and development spending while products remain in development. Comprehensive loss was $(0.116) million for the quarter and $(0.861) million for six months, including foreign currency translation impacts.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 for the three and six months ended November 30, 2025.
  • Operating loss: $(0.112) million (three months), $(0.688) million (six months).
  • Net loss: $(0.112) million (three months), $(0.857) million (six months). Includes stock-based compensation and loss on debt settlement.
  • Net comprehensive loss: $(0.116) million (three months), $(0.861) million (six months).
  • Earnings per share – basic and diluted: $(0.00) (three months), $(0.02) (six months); weighted average shares outstanding 39.20 million (six months).

Business Highlights

  • Product development: Continued software development efforts with capitalized development costs focused on building payment and personal finance platforms.
  • Product strategy: Positioning an AI-driven personal finance “super-app” combining health payment services with features such as HISA, Smart Debit Card and MoneyNet.
  • Market rollout plan: Targeting an initial launch in Canada with planned expansion into the U.S. and other global markets via subsidiaries Zane Inc CA and Zane US Inc.
  • Management & staffing: Allocated senior management and contractors to advance development; CEO and subsidiary CEO received management consulting compensation.
  • R&D investment: Increased research and development spending to support platform buildout and differentiation in the payments and personal finance space.

Original SEC Filing: PREAXIA HEALTH CARE PAYMENT SYSTEMS INC. [ PAXH ] - 10-Q - Apr. 21, 2026

data.title: Cleveland-Cliffs 10-Q: Revenues $4,922M, EPS $(0.42)
data.description:
Cleveland-Cliffs reported consolidated revenues of $4,922 million for the quarter and a net loss attributable to shareholders of $229 million, or $(0.42) per basic and diluted share, reflecting improved results versus the prior year driven by higher steel pricing and mix. The company recorded an operating loss of $213 million and reported gross results showing an implied gross deficit consistent with elevated costs. Management attributes the revenue gain primarily to higher hot‑rolled coil pricing and a favorable shift in sales mix.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $4,922 million (consolidated), up $293 million year‑over‑year.
  • Operating loss: $(213) million.
  • Operating costs (COGS, SG&A included): $5,135 million.
  • Gross profit (stated): $(82) million (implied in filing).
  • Net loss: $(229) million (includes $(3) million from discontinued operations).
  • Loss per common share attributable to shareholders: Basic $(0.42); Diluted $(0.42) (improved from $(1.01) in prior year).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and pricing: Higher average hot‑rolled coil pricing (HRC avg $1,048/nt) and a favorable shift away from slab contract volumes toward higher‑margin hot‑rolled and distributor/converter channels.
  • Channel shift: Conclusion of a slab contract resulted in sales migrating to automotive‑focused, value‑added sheet products and distributor channels, improving sales mix.
  • Operational efficiency actions: Continued footprint optimization, including idling and closures and consolidation of plate mills to improve utilization and reduce costs.
  • Market dynamics: Tariffs and reduced imports supported domestic pricing and demand, benefiting the company’s automotive and value‑added sheet product lines.
  • Sustainability & innovation: Progress on greenhouse gas reduction targets, continued HBI production initiatives in the Great Lakes region, deployment of AI in operations, and R&D trials with automotive customers.

Original SEC Filing: CLEVELAND-CLIFFS INC. [ CLF ] - 10-Q - Apr. 21, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Intuitive Surgical Q1 revenue beats estimates, helped by higher procedure volumes
data.description:
Overview

  • Robotic surgery technology firm's Q1 revenue rose 23%, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 beat analyst expectations

  • Company repurchased 2.3 mln shares for $1.1 bln in Q1

Outlook

  • Company expects 2026 da Vinci procedure growth of 13.5% to 15.5%

  • Intuitive Surgical sees 2026 non-GAAP gross profit margin of 67.5% to 68.5%

  • Company expects 2026 non-GAAP operating expense growth of 11% to 14%

Result Drivers

  • PROCEDURE VOLUME - Growth in worldwide procedure volumes, including 16% growth for da Vinci and 39% for Ion, drove higher revenue

  • SYSTEM PLACEMENTS - Increased placements of da Vinci surgical systems, especially da Vinci 5, contributed to higher systems revenue

  • INSTALLED BASE EXPANSION - Growth in the installed base of da Vinci and Ion systems supported increased recurring revenue from instruments, accessories, and services

data.title: EQT beats profit estimates on higher commodity price, sales volume
data.description:
U.S. natural gas producer EQT Corp beat Wall Street estimates for first-quarter profit on Tuesday, benefiting from higher commodity prices and output.

Surging power demand from data centers, coupled with growing liquefied natural gas exports, is underpinning U.S. natural gas consumption and encouraging producers to raise output to meet rising demand.

U.S. natural gas futures averaged $9.54 per million British thermal units (Btu) in the January-March quarter, up 9.5% from last year. Natgas prices were supported by a surge in spot prices during a winter storm early in the quarter.

EQT's average realized price for the quarter, including the impact of hedges, was $5.08 per thousand cubic feet equivalent (Mcfe), compared with $3.77 in the same period a year ago.

EQT's total quarterly sales volume was up 8.2% from a year earlier, reaching 617.69 million cubic feet equivalent (MMcfe).

The company reported an adjusted profit of $2.33 per share for the quarter ended March 31, above analysts' average estimate of $2.12 per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

data.title: Prudential's Japanese Unit Extends Sales Pause for 180 Days Amid Reform Effort
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Prudential of Japan, Prudential Financial's Japanese unit, will suspend its sales for an additional 180 days as the company looks to revamp the unit following employee misconduct issues.

The extension "reflects Prudential's conclusion that the scope and complexity of the required changes within POJ are greater than previously anticipated and will take additional time to design and implement," the company said Tuesday.

Those changes include operational, organizational, and governance changes that are needed to resume sales, the company said, adding that it initiated an independent, third-party review of Prudential of Japan that is ongoing and is expected to last several months.

Prudential of Japan Chief Executive Officer Hiromitsu Tokumaru apologized to customers for the disruption and for "falling short of the expectations we expect of ourselves."

Prudential Financial CEO Andy Sullivan added that his team is working to ensure changes made at the Japanese unit are thorough, durable, and in keeping with the overall company's standards. He said the company would not resume sales "until we were comfortable that POJ's compliance and oversight environment supports doing so."

Prudential of Japan suspended its sales for 90 days in early February, after an investigation found three former employees allegedly inappropriately handled money related to Prudential Life's systems or insurance operations, including insurance fraud, while they worked at the unit.

The company said in March that an investigation found that a group of employees obtained and shared information from agencies without permission.

Prudential said Tuesday that the Japanese unit will continue reforming its governance, sales activity conduct management, and compensation and evaluation systems during the extension. The unit is financially sound and able to meet its commitments, the company said.

Shares fell 0.9% to $95.57 in after-hours trading Tuesday. The stock closed at $96.45, down 6% for the day and 15% this year.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Weatherford International posts Q1 2026 revenue $1.15B, adjusted EBITDA $233M, net income $108M
data.description:
Weatherford International reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenues of $1,152 million and adjusted EBITDA of $233 million. The company posted net income attributable to Weatherford of $108 million and diluted EPS of $1.49 for the quarter. Management flagged near-term uncertainty from Middle East operational disruptions while maintaining full-year adjusted free cash flow guidance.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $1,152 million for Q1 2026, down 3% year-over-year and down 11% sequentially.
  • Operating income: $123 million in Q1 2026, down 13% year-over-year and down 38% sequentially.
  • Net income attributable to Weatherford: $108 million in Q1 2026; net income margin 9.4%, up 300 basis points year-over-year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA*: $233 million in Q1 2026; adjusted EBITDA margin* 20.2%, down 98 basis points year-over-year.
  • Diluted income per share: $1.49 in Q1 2026, up 44% year-over-year and down 22% sequentially.

Business Highlights

  • Awarded a multi-year Integrated Completions contract with TotalEnergies to support offshore operations in Denmark.
  • Secured a five-year Tubular Running Services (TRS) contract for offshore operations in Vietnam (Phu Quoc POC) and multiple regional contract wins across Thailand, Turkey, Kazakhstan, Brazil, Colombia, Canada, Italy, UAE and Cyprus.
  • Operational milestones and technology deployments: record extended-reach wireline logging in Saudi Arabia (29,121 ft), rigless thru-tubing sand-control gravel-pack in Saudi Arabia, Vero® One-Touch deployment in Indonesia, AlphaV casing system deployment in the Irish Sea, and ESP predictive analytics deployment in Oman.
  • Reported segment performance: DRE revenue $321M with adjusted EBITDA $72M; WCC revenue $443M with adjusted EBITDA $110M; PRI revenue $296M with adjusted EBITDA $54M, reflecting softer activity notably in Middle East/North Africa/Asia and North America.
  • Corporate actions: proposed redomestication from Ireland to the United States (Texas) expected in Q3 2026, aimed at simplifying corporate structure and reducing administrative and compliance complexity.

Original SEC Filing: Weatherford International plc [ WFRD ] - 8-K - Apr. 21, 2026

data.title: Rapport Therapeutics reports sustained seizure reductions, 22-day half-life for RAP-219 in Phase 2a follow-up
data.description:
Rapport Therapeutics reported sustained biomarker and clinical seizure reductions in the 8-week follow-up of its RAP-219 Phase 2a trial, with an updated estimated half-life of ~22 days.

Key Highlights:

  • Follow-up weeks 9–12: 80% median reduction in long episodes and 90% median reduction in clinical seizures vs baseline.
  • Follow-up weeks 13–16: 68% median reduction in long episodes and 59% median reduction in clinical seizures vs baseline.
  • Across weeks 1–16: 69% median LE reduction, 68% median clinical seizure reduction; seizure freedom 12% (weeks 1–16).
  • PK update: RAP-219 half-life revised to ~22 days; plasma levels consistent with >75% receptor occupancy in weeks 9–12 and 60–75% in weeks 13–16.
  • Safety: generally well tolerated in follow-up with two mild drug-related AEs and three SAEs deemed unrelated to RAP-219 by investigators.

Original SEC Filing: Rapport Therapeutics, Inc. [ RAPP ] - 8-K - Apr. 21, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Atlantic American Received Notice of Noncompliance
data.description:
On April 17, 2026, the company received a notice from Nasdaq that it is not in compliance with Listing Rule 5250(c)(1) due to not filing its Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025. The company has 60 days to submit a plan to regain compliance and, if accepted, may have up to 180 days from the Form 10-K due date to comply. The notice has no immediate effect on the Nasdaq listing and the company is working to file promptly.

data.title: Tesla Got a Price Target Increase. Why the Analyst Still Says Hold. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Tesla stock rose early Tuesday, bouncing back from Monday's dip, but the gains didn't last.

Shares of the electric-vehicle maker rose in premarket trading, but slid with the market, closing at $386.42, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both fell about 0.6%.

A price target boost didn't seem to help much ahead of first-quarter earnings, which Tesla is due to report on Wednesday.

Investors might be waiting for earnings before deciding what's next for Tesla stock. Wall Street is looking for earnings per share of 36 cents from sales of $22.3 billion.

Earnings might not matter as much as what Elon Musk says about robo-taxis and robots. Tesla is expected to expand its robo-taxi service, launched in Austin, Texas, in June, and unveil a new version of Tesla's humanoid robot, soon.

Ahead of earnings, Jefferies analyst Philippe Houchois raised his Tesla price target to $350 from $300. He kept his Hold rating on shares.

He believes first-quarter numbers "will show further widening of the gap between vision and execution." Tesla stock trades for about 185 times earnings expected over the coming 12 months, with investors believing AI applications such as robo-taxis will usher in a new era of earnings growth. Tesla's robo-taxi expansion, however, has gone slower than expected. Houchois isn't sure Tesla can meet its goal of dozens of cities by the end of 2026.

Failing to grow the robo-taxi business significantly this year would be a disappointment. Convincing investors on Wednesday that the robo-taxi rollout is going well could make or break Tesla stock in coming months.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Intuitive Surgical Posts Higher 1Q Sales, Profit
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

Intuitive Surgical posted higher revenue and profit for the first quarter as it placed a greater number of its da Vinci systems.

The maker of robotic surgery systems on Tuesday reported a profit of $821.5 million, or $2.28 a share, compared with $698.4 million, $1.92 cents a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings were $2.50 a share. Analysts polled by FactSet expected $2.12 a share.

Revenue rose 23% to $2.77 billion, ahead of the $2.62 billion analysts expected. The increase in revenue was driven by a growth in procedure volume, higher da Vinci system placements and an increase in the installed base of da Vinci and Ion systems, the company said.

The company placed 431 da Vinci surgical systems, compared with 367 a year ago.

The company expects worldwide da Vinci procedure growth of about 13.5% to 15.5% in 2026.

Write to Christopher Kuo at chris.kuo@wsj.com

data.title: Adobe Gets $25 Billion Stock Buyback Authorization
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Abode's board of directors authorized a new $25 billion stock buyback program.

The program, which lasts through April 30, 2030, is intended to reduce dilution from stock issuances, reduce share count over time, and maximize value for shareholders, Adobe said Tuesday.

"Our new $25 billion share repurchase authorization is a direct expression of confidence in our robust cash flow and the long-term value we are delivering to investors," Adobe Chief Financial Officer Dan Durn said.

Shares of Adobe rose 1.2% to $250.14 in after-hours trading. The stock closed down 0.6% at $247.18, 29% lower this year.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Primoris Services Corporation Schedules First Quarter 2026 Earnings Conference Call and Webcast
data.description:
DALLAS--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 21, 2026--

Primoris Services Corporation ("Primoris" or the "Company") today announced it will report first quarter 2026 financial results on Tuesday, May 5, 2026, after market close. The Company's press release will be available on the Primoris website at www.prim.com.

In conjunction with the press release, management will host a conference call and webcast on Wednesday, May 6, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. U.S. Central Time (10:00 a.m. U.S. Eastern Time), to discuss the Company's first quarter 2026 results and business outlook.

Interested parties are invited to dial-in at 1-800-715-9871, or from outside the U.S. at 1-646-307-1963, using access code: 1324356, or by asking for the Primoris conference call. A link to the webcast will be accessible from the "Investors" section of the Company's website at www.prim.com.

A replay of the conference call will be available Thursday, May 7, 2026, beginning at 5:00 p.m. U.S. Central Time for seven days. The phone number for the conference call replay is 1-800-770-2030 or, for calls from outside the U.S., 1-647-362-9199, using access code: 1324356 followed by the # key. The replay of the webcast will also be available on the Company's website following the end of the live call.

About Primoris

Primoris Services Corporation is a premier specialty contractor providing critical infrastructure services to the utility, energy, and renewables markets throughout the United States and Canada. Built on a foundation of trust, we deliver a range of engineering, construction, and maintenance services that power, connect, and enhance society. On projects spanning utility-scale solar, renewables, power delivery, communications, and transportation infrastructure, we offer unmatched value to our clients, a safe and entrepreneurial culture to our employees, and innovation and excellence to our communities. To learn more, visit www.prim.com and follow us on social media at @PrimorisServicesCorporation.

data.title: Wall Street closes choppy session lower with Middle East in focus
data.description:
Key points:
  • Three main U.S. indexes all close down ~0.6% each
  • Real Estate weakest S&P 500 sector; just Energy gains
  • Dollar edges up; U.S. crude gains >2%; bitcoin dips; gold loses >2%
  • U.S. 10-year Treasury yield rises to ~4.30%

WALL STREET CLOSES LOWER WITH IRAN IN FOCUS

Wall Street's three major equity indexes closed down about 0.6% on Tuesday as anxiety about the Middle East war resurfaced as the U.S. and Iran neared the end of their two-week ceasefire with no clarity on whether they would meet for talks.

Trading was choppy during the session as optimism waxed and waned. But after market close, President Donald Trump said he was extending the ceasefire until an Iranian proposal is submitted, and discussions are concluded.

Earlier, a senior Iranian official told Reuters that Iran could attend talks with the United States in Pakistan if Washington abandons its policy of pressure and threats and that Tehran rejects negotiations aimed at surrender. Meanwhile, Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports was an "act of war" and thus a violation of the ceasefire. Around noon, a White House official said that U.S. Vice President JD Vance had not yet departed Washington for Pakistan. But later in the afternoon the New York Times reported that his trip was on hold.

With that, jitters over the war took some attention away from a Washington hearing where Federal Reserve chief nominee Kevin Warsh said on Tuesday that he had made no promises to President Donald Trump about cutting interest rates, as he tried to assure U.S. senators considering his confirmation to lead the U.S. central bank that he would act independently of the White House while pursuing broad reforms.

In individual stocks, leading the S&P 500 gains were shares in Northern Trust Corp , which rose 8.0% after it beat Wall Street estimates for first-quarter profit, as strong markets boosted assets under custody and fee income.

Among the benchmark index's 11 major sectors, energy was the sole gainer, up 1.3%. The biggest decliners were real estate , down 1.9%, and utilities , off 1.8%.

Here is your closing snapshot:

data.title: Top News Today: Trump Extends Iran Cease-Fire Indefinitely
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

STOCKS: U.S. stocks fell as planned peace negotiations between the U.S. and Iran were delayed. However, after the market closed President Trump said that the U.S. will extend its cease-fire with Iran and continue the blockade of the country's ports until its leaders present "a unified proposal."

TREASURYS: Treasury yields rose, with the yield on the 10-year note seeing its biggest gain since March.

FOREX: The U.S. dollar strengthened.

COMMODITIES: Oil futures settled higher with U.S.-Iran talks undecided.

HEADLINES

Higher Gasoline Prices Lifted March Retail Sales

Sales at U.S. retailers jumped in March, driven by a sharp rise in gasoline prices amid the war in Iran that left Americans paying more to fill up.

Total retail sales rose by 1.7% last month, following a 0.7% increase in February. It was the fastest one-month rise in retail sales in more than three years. Analysts polled by The Wall Street Journal were expecting a 1.5% increase. The figures are adjusted for seasonal patterns but not for rising prices.

Excluding gasoline, sales were up by 0.6% month over month. Spending at electronics and appliance stores and at building-material suppliers advanced, while sales at clothing stores were flat.

United Airlines Profit Rises Despite Higher Jet Fuel Costs

United Airlines' profit grew in its latest quarter as higher sales offset added fuel costs.

The Chicago-based carrier on Tuesday posted a first-quarter profit of $699 million, or $2.14 a share, compared with $387 million, or $1.16 a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings per share were $1.19, compared with estimates of $1.08 a share according to analysts polled by FactSet.

Struggling Spirit Airlines in Talks With Trump Administration on Government Investment

Spirit Airlines may get a lifeline from the Trump administration.

President Trump on Tuesday said he was concerned about the prospect of 14,000 jobs disappearing as the struggling discounter faces the prospect of going out of business, and he suggested he would like to find a way to salvage it.

"Spirit's in trouble," he said. "Maybe the federal government should help that one out."

RTX Boosts Guidance on Defense Business Strength

RTX bumped up its full-year forecast citing the strength of its defense business.

The defense contractor also on Tuesday logged higher profit and sales in its latest quarter, with growth in each of its segments.

For the full year, RTX now projects adjusted earnings per share of $6.70 to $6.90, up from $6.60 to $6.80. Analysts polled by FactSet forecast $6.85.

New York Sues Coinbase, Gemini Over Crypto Exchanges' Prediction Markets

New York Attorney General Letitia James filed lawsuits against crypto exchanges Coinbase and Gemini, accusing them of operating prediction markets that violate state gambling laws.

The suits filed Tuesday are notable for whom they left out: Kalshi and Polymarket, which have dominated the rapidly growing world of "event contract" markets that the companies say are more akin to trading. The "yes/no" contracts allow people to bet on what might happen with everyday events, such as the economy or election results.

The cases against Coinbase and Gemini say that "each contract is a bet."

UnitedHealth Shows Signs of Turnaround With Strong Quarterly Results

UnitedHealth Group reported first-quarter results that substantially overshot Wall Street expectations and raised its annual guidance, signaling progress in its financial turnaround.

The announcement is likely to build investor confidence in the healthcare company's current direction, a year after UnitedHealth announced an earnings shortfall that touched off an unprecedented share meltdown for the company.

Former Chief Executive Stephen Hemsley returned to the top job last May, saying the company needed a reset. He has focused on retrenching and bolstering margins.

Northrop Grumman Profit Jumps on Unprecedented Demand

Northrop Grumman logged a jump in profit and higher sales in the first quarter, amid what Chief Executive Kathy Warden called an unprecedented global demand environment.

The aerospace-and-defense company on Tuesday posted a profit of $875 million, compared with $481 million a year earlier. Quarterly earnings of $6.14 a share topped the $6.05 a share that analysts polled by FactSet expected.

Total sales rose 4.4% to $9.88 billion, ahead of Wall Street models for $9.75 billion.

U.S. Pending-Home Sales Rose in March

The number of homes going under contract in the U.S. rose in March, according to a monthly index. Here are the main takeaways from the National Association of Realtors' report released Tuesday.

-The pending home sales index, a leading indicator of house sales based on contract signings, rose 1.5% on month to 73.7. Economists polled by The Wall Street Journal had forecast a 0.5% increase.

-"Contract signings rose in March despite higher mortgage rates, pointing to pent-up housing demand," said NAR Chief Economist Dr. Lawrence Yun. "A greater supply of inventory will help translate that demand into more home sales," he added.

D.R. Horton Posts Lower Profit

D.R. Horton recorded lower second-quarter profit as affordability concerns and economic uncertainty continued to put off homebuyers, forcing it to offer elevated incentives.

"Affordability constraints and cautious consumer sentiment continue to impact new home demand," said Executive Chairman David Auld.

"We expect our sales incentives to remain elevated in fiscal 2026, with incentive levels dependent on demand, mortgage interest rates and other market conditions."

Halliburton Profit Rises on Steady Demand for Services

Halliburton's first-quarter earnings rose sharply, reflecting charges from a year earlier, as demand for the oilfield outfitter's services remained steady despite a spike in crude prices late in the quarter and upheaval in major energy producing nations.

The Houston oil-services giant posted earnings of $461 million, or 55 cents a share, up from $204 million, or 24 cents a share, a year earlier. The year-earlier quarter included $356 million in impairment and other one-off charges.

Stripping out certain one-time items in the latest period, Halliburton logged first-quarter adjusted earnings of 55 cents a share, handily beating the mean analyst estimate of 50 cents a share, as per FactSet.

GE Aerospace Revenue Rises as Orders Surge

GE Aerospace posted higher first-quarter revenue on surging orders amid strong air travel and military demand.

The company reported net income from continuing operations of $1.93 billion, or $1.83 a share, compared with $1.97 billion, or $1.83 a share, the year prior.

Adjusted earnings were $1.86 a share. Analysts polled by FactSet had expected $1.60 a share.

3M Reaffirms Forecast as Macro Uncertainty Lingers

3M reaffirmed its full-year guidance, betting that rebounding demand for industrial products and office supplies will outweigh caution from consumers.

The St. Paul, Minn., manufacturing giant struck a conservative note during Tuesday's earnings call, as executives said U.S. shoppers are remaining cautious about discretionary spending.

"Given that we are early in the year and we are operating in a volatile macro environment, we think it is prudent to keep a contingency until we have more clarity about the rest of the year," Chief Financial Officer Anurag Maheshwari said.

TALKING POINT Wall Street Brings Sophisticated Quant Trading to the Masses

Wall Street's favorite new way of making money is selling sophisticated investing strategies to Main Street.

JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, Citigroup and other banks are competing to sell programs that systematically execute trades based on preset rules. Hedge funds and others have long deployed such quant-trading strategies, but now pension funds, endowments, family offices and others are embracing them.

Asset managers are also seeing a surge in interest in the strategies from wealthy investors.

Fueling interest in these trading products, which banks call quantitative investment strategies, or QIS: fear that traditional investment approaches can't keep up in the age of artificial intelligence.

"The speed of the market is increasing, and we don't have conviction about managers who mainly rely on fundamental analysis," says Elmer Huh, chief investment officer of the Murdock Trust, a foundation in the Pacific Northwest. "We think we can adapt a lot quicker with a quantitative approach."

Since December, the foundation has shifted 3% of its $2.1 billion investment portfolio to QIS funds managed by Goldman Sachs Asset Management.

Bank trading units now manage about $850 billion in QIS programs globally, up from $362 billion five years ago, according to Premialab, which helps institutions analyze systematic market-data strategies. These trades often rely on borrowed money, or leverage, to amplify returns, meaning they represent more than $1 trillion, enough to move markets, Premialab says.

  • Gregory Zuckerman

data.title: Oregon Pacific Bancorp Q1 net income falls
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. community bank's Q1 net income declined from prior quarter, diluted EPS at $0.33

  • Net interest margin expanded to 4.13%, driven by loan prepayments and fee amortization

  • Company announced CEO Ron Green's pending retirement, Amber White named successor

Outlook

  • Company expects most benefit from retired brokered CDs to be realized in Q2 2026

  • Bank anticipates continued growth in trust services noninterest income in 2026

  • Financial operations for downgraded nonprofit loan expected to stabilize, with no loss anticipated

Result Drivers

  • MARGIN EXPANSION - Net interest margin rose to 4.13%, with about half the increase attributed to nonrecurring loan prepayments and fee amortization, per company

  • LOAN PAYOFFS - Decline in outstanding loan balances was driven by $16.7 mln in early loan payoffs, including a large property sale and refinancing into FNMA programs, per company

  • HIGHER NONINTEREST EXPENSE - Noninterest expense increased, mainly due to higher salaries, bonus compensation, payroll taxes, and employee benefits, per company

data.title: Consumers Just Keep Spending. Deckers And 6 Other Retail Stocks for Your Shopping List. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Jacob Sonenshine

For all the concerns around geopolitics and oil prices, demand for retail goods continues to hold up well. Apparel stocks look particularly appealing against that backdrop.

Even with rising oil prices and concerns that central banks could even raise interest rates, "consumer spending remains resilient," writes EY-Parthenon chief economist Gregory Daco.

While data showing consumers' sentiment readings have looked bleak in both widely-cited public data and UBS' data, actual consumer spending figures point to healthy enough growth. Retail sales for March rose 4% year over year, a touch better than in February. That was the fastest growth since November.

A UBS survey shows U.S. consumers plan to spend 4.4% more on apparel and footwear in the next 90 days versus the same period last year.

"U.S. consumers' view of the economy has been somewhat impacted month over month by the Middle East conflict and rising gas prices," writes UBS analyst Jay Sole. "Yet, the data show potential for a strong pop in U.S. consumer spending."

That makes retail stocks look attractive. The market is under appreciating solid fundamentals for the industry, Sole writes, after years of underperformance versus the S&P 500. Stocks in his coverage universe trade at an average of just over 16 times expected earnings for the coming 12 months, a discount to the S&P 500's just over 21 times. Historically, his group averages a 9% premium to the index.

This means satisfactory or better sales and earnings results versus expectations could bring the stocks higher, especially if the earnings continue to grow for a few more years. Sole likes On Holdings, Gildan Activewear, Burlington Stores, TJX Companies, Levi Strauss, and Signet Jewelers.

Deckers Outdoor, which owns catchy brands such as Uggs and Hoka, also looks attractive. Trading at just over 15 times earnings, versus a 21 times average the past five years.

It's got plenty of growth potential. While the double digit annual sales growth it recorded from 2018 to 2025 will almost certainly slow, the company has demonstrated it can meet consumers' wants and take market share from competitors. Analysts expect 6% annual sales growth for the next three years, according to FactSet, a strong figure in this environment. Hoka, which has taken share from Nike, is expected to drive much of the growth.

The company said on its January first quarter earnings call that, while it's facing higher costs, it's also lifting prices on some products. It spoke about recently strong "full-priced" selling of Hoka. This can help it protect profit margins. Analysts forecast operating margins will remain roughly constant over the next few years.

These are results investors can believe in, given that Deckers could command strong pricing power — and that it usually beats expectations. In the past 20 quarters, it has missed revenue estimates only once, and has missed bottom line forecasts only once.

Take a chance on some of these names.

Write to Jacob Sonenshine at jacob.sonenshine@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Prudential Financial Stock Is Falling on Report of Potential Misconduct at Japan Insurer — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Janet H. Cho

Prudential Financial Inc. stock is falling on Tuesday after a report about potential employee misconduct at Japan-based Gibraltar Life Insurance.

Japan's Nikkei.com news site reported Tuesday that "dozens of cases of suspected financial fraud by former employees of Gibraltar Life Insurance, a subsidiary of the Prudential Group have surfaced."

To "prevent recurrence," Prudential Life Insurance is considering extending the period during which it will suspend new contracts, which was set to end on May 9, by about six months, the report said, in an English language translation.

The report also said "Prudential Holdings Japan, the parent company, will make an announcement soon" as external experts investigate the extent of the damage.

Prudential Financial could not immediately be reached.

Shares of Prudential Financial were down 6% on Tuesday.

The Asahi Shimbun newspaper has reported that 107 current and former Prudential Life Insurance employees "obtained money through fraudulent means or failed to repay funds they had borrowed from roughly 500 customers," amounting to an estimated 3.14 billion yen ($20 million) between 1991 and 2025, citing the company's internal investigation released on Jan. 16.

A separate investigation found that "69 employees violated internal rules by taking kickbacks for referring customers to outside investment firms," The Asahi Shimbun reported.

In early February, Prudential announced a voluntary 90-day suspension of new sales activity to support Prudential of Japan's implementation of comprehensive operational, organizational, and governance changes to address the previously disclosed incidents of misconduct by employees.

On April 9, the newspaper said that Japan's Financial Services Agency was expanding its investigation and conducting an on-site inspection of the parent company of Prudential Life Insurance Co. and examining whether "an excessive performance-driven culture that fostered misconduct and lax oversight of sales employees" contributed to what happened.

Prudential shares had traded as low as $95.74 on Tuesday afternoon, down 6.7% and on pace for its largest percentage decrease since April 4, 2025, when shares fell 7.9%, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

Shares closed down 6% at $96.45 on Tuesday. Prudential's stock is down 14.6% this year and down 3.8% over the past 12 months.

Write to Janet H. Cho at janet.cho@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Chubb Logs Higher 1Q Profit on Rising Premiums
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Chubb posted a higher profit in the first quarter as the company's net premiums for property and casualty rose.

The insurance company recorded a profit of $2.32 billion, or $5.88 a share, compared with its profit of $1.33 billion, or $3.29 a share, a year earlier.

Core operating income per share, which excludes certain items including adjusted net realized gains and market risk benefits gains, was $6.82 a share. Analysts surveyed by FactSet were expecting $6.60 a share.

Net premiums rose to $14.01 billion, up from $12.65 billion a year prior. Analysts were expecting $13.56 billion.

Property and casualty net premiums rose 7.2% to $11.72 billion, with consumer insurance premiums rising 14% and commercial insurance up 4.6%.

Life insurance net premiums rose 33% to $2.29 billion.

Chief Executive Officer Evan Greenberg said the company did not renew a "substantial percentage" of the its shared and layered property business in response to rapidly softening property market conditions. He added the financial lines insurance market is currently soft.

Greenberg also said that the company was relatively insulated from the war in Iran.

Chubb is currently serving as lead underwriter for the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation's Maritime Reinsurance facility, which insures ships traveling through the Strait of Hormuz. Chubb and other insurers on the policy doubled its coverage to $40 billion earlier this month.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

There is no immediate impact on trading; the company has until June 15, 2026 to file or submit a compliance plan, with a potential exception period extending to October 12, 2026.
